rag.description: The ConvertToBase64 plugin encodes text or expression values to Base64 during automation execution. It optionally applies a regular expression to the argument before encoding, capturing only the first matched portion. The encoded result is stored in the session parameter ConvertToBase64:Result and exposed in the response entity under the key Result for downstream use.

qa.question: What does the ConvertToBase64 plugin do?
qa.answer: It takes the value in the Argument property, optionally filters it with a RegularExpression, and converts the result to a Base64-encoded string stored in the session parameter ConvertToBase64:Result.

qa.question: How does the RegularExpression property affect encoding?
qa.answer: The regex is applied to the argument before encoding. Only the first match is encoded. If the regex matches nothing, an empty string is encoded. Omit it or use (.*) to encode the full argument value.

qa.question: Where is the encoded result available after the plugin runs?
qa.answer: The Base64-encoded value is stored in the session parameter ConvertToBase64:Result and is also accessible through the response entity under the key Result.

qa.question: What does the default RegularExpression value (si).* do?
qa.answer: The default value (si).* is a case-insensitive, single-line pattern that matches the entire argument value. Omitting RegularExpression or leaving it at its default encodes the full argument without any filtering.

description: ### Purpose
Converts text or expression values to Base64 for safe transport, storage, and downstream reuse.
It takes the value of the `Argument` property, optionally filters it with a `RegularExpression`, and encodes the result.
The encoded output is stored in the session as `ConvertToBase64:Result` and exposed through the response entity.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature             | Description                                                                                 |
| Base64 Encoding     | Converts the `Argument` value to a Base64 string.                                           |
| Regex Pre-filtering | Applies `RegularExpression` to extract a specific portion of the argument before encoding.  |
| Session Output      | Stores the encoded result in the session as `ConvertToBase64:Result` for downstream access. |
### Usages in RPA
| Use Case            | Description                                                                                    |
| Credential Encoding | Encode a username or password to Base64 for use in HTTP Basic Auth headers.                    |
| Data Transport      | Encode structured text before passing it to a downstream API or system requiring Base64 input. |
| Partial Extraction  | Use a regex to isolate a specific field from a longer string and encode only that portion.     |
### Usages in Automation Testing
| Use Case            | Description                                                                                    |
| Header Construction | Build Base64-encoded authorization headers for API test steps without manual encoding.         |
| Value Verification  | Encode expected values to compare against Base64-encoded API responses in assertions.          |
| Selective Encoding  | Extract a substring with regex and encode it for use in parameterized test data or assertions. |
Contains the Base64-encoded string produced from the argument after optional regex pre-filtering.
The value is available in the response entity under the key `Result` and also stored in the session as `ConvertToBase64:Result`.
entity.name: Result

edge_cases: Empty argument produces an empty Base64 string
Argument containing special characters is encoded without alteration
Expression argument is resolved at runtime before encoding
annotations.expected_result: The string “Hello, World!” is encoded to Base64 and stored in ConvertToBase64:Result.
annotations.notes: No RegularExpression is specified so the full argument value is encoded.

description: ### Encode a Static String to Base64
Encode a fixed text value to Base64 during automation execution.
It uses the `ConvertToBase64` plugin with the argument set to `Hello, World!`.
No `RegularExpression` is specified, so the full argument value is encoded.
The result is stored in the session as `ConvertToBase64:Result`.

edge_cases: Regex matches nothing — empty string is encoded
Multiple numeric sequences present — only the first match is encoded
Non-numeric argument — regex produces no match, result is an empty Base64 string
annotations.expected_result: The numeric portion “12345” is extracted from the argument and encoded to Base64, stored in ConvertToBase64:Result.
annotations.notes: Only the first regex match is encoded. If the pattern matches nothing, an empty string is encoded.

description: ### Encode a Regex-Matched Portion to Base64
Extract a specific part of a string with a regular expression and encode only that portion.
It uses the `ConvertToBase64` plugin with the argument `Order-12345-Details` and `regularExpression` set to `\d+`.
The first numeric sequence `12345` is matched and encoded to Base64.
The result is stored in the session as `ConvertToBase64:Result`.

edge_cases: Session parameter does not exist — the expression resolves to an empty string which is then encoded
Session parameter contains special characters — the full resolved value is encoded without alteration
annotations.expected_result: The runtime value of the Username session parameter is resolved and encoded to Base64, stored in ConvertToBase64:Result.
annotations.notes: The argument is a G4 expression that resolves the session parameter value at runtime before encoding. No RegularExpression is specified so the full resolved value is encoded.

description: ### Encode a Session Parameter Value to Base64
Encode the runtime value of a session parameter to Base64 during automation execution.
It uses the `ConvertToBase64` plugin with the argument set to `{{$Get-Parameter --Name:Username --Scope:Session}}`.
The G4 expression is resolved at runtime, retrieving the current value of the `Username` session parameter before encoding.
No `RegularExpression` is specified, so the full resolved value is encoded and stored in the session as `ConvertToBase64:Result`.
The session parameter that holds the Base64-encoded result.
The parameter contains the encoded value produced from the argument after optional regex pre-filtering.
Its value can be referenced by other parameters or expressions in downstream workflow steps.
outputParameters.name: ConvertToBase64:Result

description: The value to encode to Base64.
Accepts a fixed string or a G4 expression that resolves at runtime.
If a `RegularExpression` is also set, only the first matched portion of this value is encoded.
properties.name: Argument

description: A regular expression applied to the `Argument` before encoding.
Only the first match is encoded; if there is no match, an empty string is encoded.
Omit this property or use `(si).*` to encode the entire argument value.
properties.name: RegularExpression

summary: Encodes text or expression values to Base64 for safe transport and storage.
Applies an optional regular expression to the argument before encoding, capturing only the first matched portion.
Stores the encoded result in the session as ConvertToBase64:Result and exposes it through the response entity.
